text: The surgeon uses a supraumbilical cut down and inserts a blunt trocar into the abdominal cavity, taking care to avoid injury to the dilated and fragile bowel. The ports are inserted under direct vision, with the open jaws of the hands ensuring safe entry. The division of adhesions is performed with scissors, keeping the tip pointed towards the abdominal wall to prevent enterotomy. The surgeon identifies the transition point between the collapsed and dilated loops of the small bowel, noting the pale mucosa of the collapsed bowel and the hyperemic red mucosa of the dilated bowel. A band has been divided, clearly demonstrating the transition point.

The surgeon assesses the bowel viability on both sides of the constriction ring, indicating a positive outcome. The CT scan revealed a closed loop obstruction, prompting the search for a second transition point. Upon examination, the proximal bowel shows a thickened and scarred mesentery near the SMA and SMV pedicle, with the bowel twisted around it. The twist is unraveled, exposing the scarred area, but further dissection is avoided to prevent compromising blood supply. Additional adhesions are carefully removed to prevent future complications.
